description: Fix broken web deployment pipeline, replace Azure client secrets with OIDC, adopt FirebaseUI for login, add Tailwind CSS, and restyle all pages

userStories.title: Add secrets-management rule to CLAUDE.md
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CLAUDE.md to include a secrets-management rule so that all future development follows secure practices.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a new '## Secrets & Security' section to the end of CLAUDE.md in the repo root
Rule states: 'Store all secrets in Azure Key Vault (AKV). Avoid passwords and client secrets wherever possible; prefer managed identity and federated credentials.'
References Key Vaults: buddyburn-beta-kv (beta) and buddyburn-prod-kv (production)
Mentions that firebase-api-key and firebase-auth-domain can be retrieved from AKV as needed
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File is at /workspaces/burnbuddy/CLAUDE.md. Append the new section after the existing '## Commands' section.

userStories.title: Fix deploy-web.yml — gate prod on beta E2E
userStories.description: As a developer, I want production web deployments to only proceed if beta E2E tests pass, so that broken code never reaches production.
acceptanceCriteria: In .github/workflows/deploy-web.yml, change deploy-prod job's 'needs' from 'deploy-beta' to 'test-beta'
The pipeline dependency chain is now: quality → deploy-beta → test-beta → deploy-prod → test-prod
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Currently line 146 says 'needs: deploy-beta'. Change to 'needs: test-beta'. This is a one-line fix. The API workflow (deploy-api.yml) already has this correct (its deploy-prod needs test-beta).

userStories.title: Update deploy-web.yml for OIDC federated auth
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the web deployment workflow to use OIDC workload identity federation instead of client secrets for Azure authentication.
acceptanceCriteria: Add top-level 'permissions:' block with 'id-token: write' and 'contents: read' to the workflow

Replace all azure/login@v2 steps that use 'creds' JSON containing clientSecret with OIDC inputs: client-id (${{ secrets.AZURE_CLIENT_ID }}), tenant-id (${{ secrets.AZURE_TENANT_ID }}), subscription-id (${{ secrets.AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID }})
Remove all references to AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET from deploy-web.yml
There are 4 azure/login steps in deploy-web.yml (deploy-beta, test-beta, deploy-prod, test-prod) — update all 4
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: OIDC login format for azure/login@v2: use 'client-id', 'tenant-id', 'subscription-id' as separate inputs (not the 'creds' JSON). Federated credentials must be pre-configured on the Azure AD app registration for repo:patelr3/burnbuddy:environment:beta and :production.

userStories.title: Update deploy-api.yml for OIDC federated auth
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the API deployment workflow to use OIDC workload identity federation instead of client secrets.
acceptanceCriteria: Add top-level 'permissions:' block with 'id-token: write' and 'contents: read' to the workflow
Replace all azure/login@v2 steps that use 'creds' JSON containing clientSecret with OIDC inputs: client-id, tenant-id, subscription-id
Remove all references to AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET from deploy-api.yml
There are 4 azure/login steps in deploy-api.yml (deploy-beta, test-beta, deploy-prod, test-prod) — update all 4
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Same pattern as US-003 but for .github/workflows/deploy-api.yml. Use identical OIDC input format.

userStories.title: Install and configure Tailwind CSS
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Tailwind CSS installed in the web app so that pages can use utility-first styling.
acceptanceCriteria: Install tailwindcss, @tailwindcss/postcss, and postcss as dev dependencies in apps/web (use yarn workspace burnbuddy-web add -D)
Create apps/web/tailwind.config.ts with content paths: ['./src/**/*.{ts,tsx}']
Create apps/web/postcss.config.js with @tailwindcss/postcss plugin
Create apps/web/src/app/globals.css with Tailwind directives: @tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities;
Import globals.css in apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx
Define BurnBuddy color palette in tailwind config: primary orange #f97316, secondary blue #3b82f6, success green #22c55e, danger red #ef4444
Add a test class (e.g. className='text-primary') to verify it works
yarn build succeeds in apps/web
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The web app currently has zero CSS files — all styling is inline. Tailwind will coexist with inline styles so migration can be incremental. Check package name: the web app workspace might be 'burnbuddy-web' or '@burnbuddy/web' — verify in apps/web/package.json.

userStories.title: Update deploy-web.yml to succeed with OIDC and Tailwind changes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the deploy-web.yml workflow to succeed
acceptanceCriteria: Trigger the GitHub Actions workflow and keep fixing issues until it succeeds
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: It was originally failing due to missing AKV secrets user

userStories.title: Replace login page with FirebaseUI widget
userStories.description: As a user, I want a polished login page using the official FirebaseUI drop-in auth widget.
acceptanceCriteria: Install firebaseui and react-firebaseui packages in apps/web
Replace the custom form in apps/web/src/app/login/page.tsx with StyledFirebaseAuth component
Configure FirebaseUI with EmailAuthProvider and GoogleAuthProvider sign-in options
Import firebaseui CSS globally (in layout.tsx or the login page)
Page has 'use client' directive
After successful auth, redirect to home page '/'
Page has BurnBuddy branding (logo/title above the widget)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-005 (Tailwind for surrounding page styling). FirebaseUI handles email/password login AND signup in one widget, so a separate signup page may become optional.

userStories.title: Update signup page for FirebaseUI flow
userStories.description: As a user, I want the signup page to integrate with or redirect to the FirebaseUI login widget.
acceptanceCriteria: Update apps/web/src/app/signup/page.tsx to redirect to /login since FirebaseUI handles both login and signup
Or: replace signup page content with a note and link to /login ('Sign up on our login page')
Remove old custom signup form code
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-006 (FirebaseUI login page). Since FirebaseUI handles registration, the separate signup page becomes a redirect.

userStories.title: Extract and restyle shared nav bar component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a shared nav bar component styled with Tailwind that all pages can use.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/components/NavBar.tsx as a 'use client' component
Extract the nav bar markup from the home page (apps/web/src/app/page.tsx, approximately lines 259-287)
Nav bar includes: BurnBuddy logo/text, links to Friends and Account, Sign Out button
Style with Tailwind: sticky top-0, white background, shadow-sm, flex layout, orange brand color for active link
Sign out button calls Firebase signOut and redirects to /login
Replace the inline nav in the home page with the new NavBar component import
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-005 (Tailwind). The nav bar is currently duplicated inline in each page with inline styles. Extract once, then subsequent page restyles will import it. Use Next.js usePathname() for active link highlighting.

userStories.title: Restyle home/dashboard page with Tailwind
userStories.description: As a user, I want the home page to look modern and polished with consistent card styling.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace ALL inline style props in apps/web/src/app/page.tsx with Tailwind utility classes
Use the shared NavBar component (from US-008) instead of inline nav
Active workout banner: rounded-lg, shadow, yellow/amber background, clear typography hierarchy
Start Workout button: bg-orange-500 hover:bg-orange-600, rounded-lg, font-semibold, padding
Buddy and squad cards: border, rounded-lg, shadow-sm, consistent padding, hover effect
Container: max-w-xl mx-auto px-4
Responsive: looks good at 375px mobile and 1280px desktop
No inline style props remain in the file
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-008 (shared NavBar). This is the largest page (675 lines). Focus on replacing style objects with className strings. Keep the same layout structure — just swap the styling approach.

userStories.title: Restyle friends page with Tailwind
userStories.description: As a user, I want the friends page to look polished with clear visual hierarchy.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace ALL inline style props in apps/web/src/app/friends/page.tsx with Tailwind classes
Use the shared NavBar component instead of inline nav
Friend list items: card-style with rounded border, padding, name and action buttons
Add/search section clearly separated with border-t or bg-gray-50
Empty state: centered text with friendly message
Responsive layout
No inline style props remain
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-005 and US-008. Page is 425 lines.

userStories.title: Restyle account page with Tailwind
userStories.description: As a user, I want the account settings page to look clean and organized.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace ALL inline style props in apps/web/src/app/account/page.tsx with Tailwind classes
Use the shared NavBar component instead of inline nav
Form fields: consistent w-full, rounded-md border, focus:ring-orange-500
Sign out button: styled as destructive (text-red-600 or bg-red-500 text-white)
Container: max-w-xl mx-auto px-4
Responsive layout
No inline style props remain
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-005 and US-008. Page is 183 lines — small.

userStories.title: Restyle burn buddy pages with Tailwind
userStories.description: As a user, I want the burn buddy creation and detail pages to look polished.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace ALL inline style props in apps/web/src/app/burn-buddies/new/page.tsx with Tailwind classes
If apps/web/src/app/burn-buddies/[id]/page.tsx exists, restyle it too
Use the shared NavBar component instead of inline nav
Form fields: consistent sizing, labels, rounded-md, focus ring
Submit button: bg-green-500 hover:bg-green-600, rounded, font-semibold
Detail view (if exists): card layout with burn streak, action buttons styled
Responsive layout
No inline style props remain in either file
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-005 and US-008. new page is 170 lines. Detail page may or may not exist — check and skip if absent.

userStories.title: Restyle burn squad pages with Tailwind
userStories.description: As a user, I want the burn squad creation and detail pages to look polished.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace ALL inline style props in apps/web/src/app/burn-squads/new/page.tsx with Tailwind classes
If apps/web/src/app/burn-squads/[id]/page.tsx exists, restyle it too
Use the shared NavBar component instead of inline nav
Form fields: consistent sizing, labels, rounded-md, focus ring
Submit button: bg-blue-500 hover:bg-blue-600, rounded, font-semibold
Detail view (if exists): card layout with member list, burn stats, action buttons styled
Responsive layout
No inline style props remain in either file
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-005 and US-008. new page is 272 lines. Detail page may or may not exist — check and skip if absent.

userStories.title: Verify deploy-api.yml still succeeds
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the deploy-api.yml workflow to succeed
acceptanceCriteria: Trigger the GitHub Actions workflow and keep fixing issues until it succeeds
Typecheck passes
